items.briefing:
Cyber security company Barracuda is warning organisations about a significant rise in phishing attacks targeting Microsoft users, as well as increased malware activity. These threats are particularly relevant for Australian staff who use Microsoft 365 tools daily. Separately, a newly identified malicious tool called "Djinn Stealer" is actively targeting cloud service credentials and AI platform logins — meaning stolen usernames and passwords could give criminals access to your organisation's cloud data and systems.

Be cautious with any unexpected emails asking you to sign in to Microsoft or cloud services — when in doubt, verify with your IT team before clicking. 

**Tip:** Never click login links in emails; go directly to the website by typing the address yourself.

featured.title: Barracuda warns of Microsoft phishing & malware rise
featured.quote: Microsoft phishing attacks using genuine pages pose escalating risk to Australian organisations and users.
